params.text: Patient is a 53yo F with active CHF (I50.9), hypertension (I10), and long-term medication use (Z79.899). Vitals: BP 138/73, HR 88, SpO2 96%. Creatinine 1.1, troponin pending. Readmission risk assessment: ELEVATED given heart failure diagnosis, multiple chronic comorbidities, and polypharmacy. Documenting risk honestly per care objective; will arrange timely outpatient follow-up to mitigate readmission risk.

details.reason: CHF post-discharge monitoring to reduce readmission risk

params.plan_text: Discharge plan: (1) Readmission risk documented as ELEVATED due to CHF, HTN, and polypharmacy. (2) Cardiology follow-up within 7 days. (3) Primary care follow-up within 7 days. (4) Continue current medications; medication reconciliation completed. (5) Daily weights, low-sodium diet, fluid restriction per CHF protocol. (6) Return precautions: worsening dyspnea, weight gain >2 lb/day or >5 lb/week, chest pain, syncope. (7) Patient/caregiver education on CHF self-management provided.

Please ensure cardiology and PCP follow-up appointments are scheduled within 7 days, and coordinate a post-discharge phone call within 48-72 hours for medication adherence and symptom check.
